posts.author: rdvs34283
posts.title: Top Dating Site OKCupid Gave Millions of User Photos to AI Firm Without Consent. FTC: the decision was influenced by financial ties, as OkCupid’s founders had invested in the AI company.

link_metadata.description: Nearly three million OkCupid users uploaded photos expecting them to remain on a dating platform. Instead, those images were transferred to an artificial intelligence company and used to train facial recognition systems, without user consent, according to federal regulators.
link_metadata.title: FTC Settlement Reveals Top Dating Site Gave Millions of User Photos to AI Firm Without Consent - Slay News

posts.raw_content:
I would like to reply to the premise of this post earlier today, by u/-f-b-i- and (while not expressly stated, I am reasonably presuming) also on behalf of the mods of PDW. 

https://patriots.win/p/1ASZMNUjjO/a-visit-from-the-feds/c/4eblBmuY4EG?d=50

I have been a part of this community since the golden escalator in summer 2015. Even as a libertarian, who has been without a formal party "home" since the trust-fund anarchist filth took over the LP, I have found great solidarity with the MAGA conservative movement. I have believed in the President's conservative populist message, from the first moments of his first campaign, and I have voted for him in 2016, 2020, and 2024. I do not regret any of my votes for him.

However, in light of the changes in behavior from the proffered platform of not 1.5 years ago, which is objectively true and observable by everyone but the most dedicated head-in-the-sand ostrich, we need to have a open dialogue about something: If Rule 1 will not be changed, as u/-f-b-i- said it won’t be, *what does it mean now?* How do we keep to our principles, not become devolved into sycophants, and defend our President from being led astray by third-party interests? Let’s dissect the Rules:

The Preamble states “Patriots is not a catch-all free speech community for the political right. We are explicitly *for supporters of MAGA and Trump*, and we facilitate a platform where *those users specifically* can speak their mind.”

Rule 1 states, “Be here for MAGA and Trump: We’re a community for those *who want to Make America Great Again.* If your purpose here is to push other unrelated or tangential agendas, you’re not welcome.”

I read these Rules textually, as allowing for two camps to be allowed here and which are permitted to coexist: (1) Those who support the Make America Great Again agenda specifically, as a platform, and (2) those who support President Trump in all of his decisionmaking as an individual. In an ideal world, there would be no difference between the two, but inevitably things change and so do people. That is why our mods making this distinction of PDW being “for MAGA *and* Trump” baked into the Rules was a wise look to the future, in case that President Trump, for whatever reasons good or ill, were to start making policy changes not in line with his historic MAGA platform. At the end of the day, leaders have to adapt to changes in the political and socioeconomic environment because not everything is predictable. That’s just a fact of governing. But it should not be mistaken as against the Rules to call out the President for controverting his own historic platform and trying to bring him back onto the path. This forum, this website, is a unique place: We know that upper levels of the administration, and its enemies alike, look to this place and to our discussions to “gauge the winds” of our community’s attitudes towards topics and developments. We are on a stage here where we can change minds. That requires we must do our part to represent the community well, and to fight for the MAGA agenda.

Now to a confusion of our semantics that should be addressed: 
In his/her post, u/-f-b-i- said the following: “This site is the last bastion in the world where you can support Trump *and America First* and this will not change. We are not going to update the rules to allow open Trump hating and we will be enforcing any overt anti Trump rhetoric with due diligence. There is no quarter for those who would have rather had an auto pen owned by Soros in office. *We are America First,* we will *Make America Great Again* even if I have to do it all myself while holding pepe's hand.”
There currently exists a legitimate debate what “America First” means. Both are fought for with equal vigor by both camps. One is a synonym of “America First means the same as Make America Great Again,” and one is a literal definition of “American interests and people must come First, before any other country on Earth, without any exception whatsoever.” I argue this should be formally clarified, as a matter of forum policy.

In closing, while I agree with what u/-f-b-i- said, I also believe there can be improvement that will increase the resilience and good faith of this forum, as well as preserve our principles. I therefore suggest the following, as some ideas for improvement in line with that post’s concerns:

First, it is time that we clarify the placement of “America First” supporting persons within the context of Rule 1. Is “America First” now what we call “old MAGA platform” and whatever is President Trump’s current position on things at any given time the “new MAGA?” Some sort of hybrid, or something else entirely? If we are going to continue to be a voice for the MAGA community here online, in this unique and special place we have, we should be on the same page as to how the forum considers the America First agenda in comparison (be it difference or similarity) to the MAGA platform and/or to President Trump's actions taken as an individual leader.

Secondly, it is time we reactivate the downvote buttons. If it is against Rule 2 to do “forum sliding, consensus cracking, topic dilution,” then any stifling of our ability to downvote posts and comments we disagree with should be considered a violation of the spirit of that Rule as well. If turning them off was a decision by someone higher up at Scored and it was not the PDW mods specifically, then that's understandable but it still  should be plainly stated. But the fact of the matter remains: Turning them off has justifiably caused extreme suspicion that some people were flying “too close to the sun,” and trust amongst us needs to be reestablished by restoring our ability to express disagreement. I have personally been extremely bothered by them being turned off ever since it happened, as it has taken away one of our core abilities to self-police outside of hard Deports for Rule violations. We need to be able to display dissent, not just praise. I also preemptively suggest that avoiding brigading is not a good enough excuse to keep them off, if turning them off here was by choice.

Third, Rule 6 says we must follow the Content Policy of Scored, and that expressly says that “Content which violates U.S. federal, state, or local law is not permitted…This includes, but is not limited to, illegal harassment, unlawful threats, and incitement to lawless action as defined by *Brandenburg v. Ohio*.” The pro-lsraeli shilling and forum manipulation from Unit 8200 et al. and their dispensationalist allies in the past couple months, even if still laughably impotent and annoying, is still not a Rule violation under the current iteration (except when used for consensus cracking because people inconveniently start agreeing on something against their government's interests). BUT, those shills are not *nearly* as imminently dangerous as the remaining “boogaloos” here still making comments trying to insinuate acceleration to civil war, and wanting to do terrible violent crimes to our “domestic enemies” out of a sense of sick vengeance. Perhaps it might not be now, but in a more heated political environment I think some of those comments will meet the *Bradenburg* Test. I therefore suggest that the “Free Chopper Rides” flair (glorifying the Kissinger-planted dictator Pinochet) should be deleted, and anyone attempting to incite violence by referencing “feeding people into woodchippers” or committing murder or torture, of even the most vile of leftists, should be formally deemed a Deport-able offense.

I think that if we do those things now, it can only help make this forum better. These are just my opinions though, just one voice out of many here that care, so I always welcome tempering of my thoughts.
